# Threshold Calibration Report
**Date:** Auto-generated by `calibrate_thresholds.py`  
**Dataset:** `artifacts/district_master_enriched.geojson` (556 districts)

---

## Why Recalibration Was Needed

The original severity buckets used fixed absolute thresholds derived from the raw
`water_level_above_danger_m` values. Because the flood dataset has a **right-skewed distribution**
(most districts have elevated water levels), nearly all 556 districts were classified as
`Critical` or `High`, making the dashboard operationally misleading for a command center audience.

**Before recalibration** (`flood_severity` field, as stored in GeoJSON):

| Severity | Count | % |
|----------|-------|---|
| Low | 50 | 9.0% |
| Moderate | 3 | 0.5% |
| High | 6 | 1.1% |
| Critical | 497 | 89.4% |

---

## Hard Operational Threshold (UNCHANGED)

> **`water_level_above_danger_m > 1.5m`**

This is a **required operational rule** (FIN100 assignment requirement).  
The AI assistant uses this exact threshold when answering queries like:  
*"Which districts have water levels exceeding the danger line by more than 1.5 meters?"*

**This threshold is locked and must never be changed.**

Districts currently exceeding this hard threshold: **497** of 556

---

## Display Severity Tier Recalibration

The dashboard **display labels** (map color, table badge, filter dropdown) have been recalibrated
to use **percentile-based thresholds** so that the distribution is operationally meaningful:

| Display Tier | Percentile | Score Range | Target % |
|-------------|------------|-------------|----------|
| 🟢 Low | P0 – P40 | ≤ 26.04 | ~40% |
| 🟡 Moderate | P40 – P70 | 26.04 – 31.76 | ~30% |
| 🟠 High | P70 – P90 | 31.76 – 44.57 | ~20% |
| 🔴 Critical | P90 – P100 | > 44.57 | ~10% |

**After recalibration** (simulated, based on `overall_risk_score`):

| Display Tier | Count | % |
|-------------|-------|---|
| Low | 224 | 40.3% |
| Moderate | 165 | 29.7% |
| High | 111 | 20.0% |
| Critical | 56 | 10.1% |

---

## Key Distinction

| Rule Type | Threshold | Purpose | Changeable? |
|-----------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| Hard Operational | `water_level_above_danger_m > 1.5m` | AI query engine, FIN100 requirement | ❌ Never |
| Display Severity | Percentile-based `overall_risk_score` | Dashboard map/table coloring | ✅ Recalibrate anytime |

---

## Files Modified

- `artifacts/thresholds.json` — Updated with `display_severity_tiers` section + hard operational section
- `docs/CALIBRATION_REPORT.md` — This report

---

## Recommendation

Run `python calibrate_thresholds.py` any time the dataset changes to regenerate
percentile-based display thresholds automatically.
